meta.title: Mariano Luna
meta.description: Mariano Luna's interactive 3D portfolio. BIM architect and fullstack developer focused on AEC workflows, automation, and web tools.

brand.aboutText:
I am an Architect with experience in building design and site management, architectural visualization and 3D, BIM modeling and coordination. Over time I evolved toward Fullstack Development, specializing in this area. I see a shared essence between constructing buildings and writing code: both require design, planning, and structured problem solving.

My transition into development started by building automation tools (Python, Dynamo) and BIM web apps (IFC.js, Three.js). Today I apply that same engineering mindset to build modern applications with the MERN stack, Next.js, and AI-assisted workflows.
brand.aboutStatement: I bring a multidisciplinary perspective that connects digital construction with software architecture, designing robust solutions from database to interface.

links.label: Projects
links.description: Applications and tools I have built.

links.label: Education
links.description: Academic background, certifications, and continuous learning applied to BIM and development.

links.description: Book a meeting with me based on availability (15, 30, or 60 minutes).

hero.subtitle: My approach combines an analytical profile with a strong digital mindset. I handle projects from spatial conception through modeling, management, and automation. I specialize in the AECO ecosystem and in building tools with tangible impact on decision-making and time optimization. I am not a programmer in the strict sense: I am a professional who uses programming to build solutions that integrate design, data, digitalization, and AI.

items.name: Control Manager
items.summary: Quality control plugin for Autodesk Revit
items.description: Quality Control plugin for Autodesk Revit for BIM coordinators: automates QC checks on the local model and delivers actionable results with no cloud services.
items.context: Revit quality reviews—empty names or type names, blank Comments, unclosed rooms, duplicate Marks, and elements without a level—are often done manually, inconsistently, and with little traceability before the model is published.
items.impact: Cuts coordination time through automatic issue detection, in-model element selection, and Excel export with priority levels (high, medium, low) for tracking. Reviews run fully on the local model, suitable when models cannot be sent to external services.
items.role: Designed and built the multi-version plugin (Revit 2023–2027): five severity-rated validation rules, WPF UI with MVVM, ClosedXML export, ribbon tab, privacy/EULA flow, and distribution via ApplicationPlugins bundle, ZIP releases, and a single-file EXE installer.
items.platform: Application plugin for Autodesk Revit 2023, 2024, 2025, 2026, and 2027 on Windows 10/11 (64-bit); .NET Framework 4.8 (2023–2026) and .NET 10 (2027).
items.scope: v1.0.0 shipped on GitHub Releases: five QC checks, manual install (ZIP) or toggle EXE installer. Autodesk App Store publication under review.

items.name: Ribbon Revit
items.summary: Ribbon in pyRevit to create scripts with the API
items.description: pyRevit extension with the ARQFI ribbon tab: organized examples to practice transactions, parameters, selection, FilteredElementCollector, dialogs, views, sheets, and graphic overrides on the active model.
items.context: Learning the Revit API often means scattered scripts that are hard to find and share, with no consistent ribbon structure or examples grouped by topic.
items.impact: Keeps learning inside Revit with tools grouped by theme, reusable helpers in lib/Snippets, and quick links to Learn Revit API, Revit API Docs, and pyRevit developer docs—less setup time before trying each API concept.
items.role: Structured and maintained the learnrevitapi.extension (ARQFI.tab, Resources/Buttons/Select/Parameters/Transactions/Collector/InOut/Views/Overrides panels, bundle.yaml), per-button scripts, and lib/Code and lib/Snippets with helpers for transactions, parameters, selection, and unit conversion.
items.platform: pyRevit extension for Autodesk Revit 2022, 2023, and 2024; Python via your pyRevit runtime (IronPython or CPython).
items.scope: Open-source educational material (MIT) on GitHub, no published releases; install by copying learnrevitapi.extension into your pyRevit extensions folder. Intended for test project copies, not production models.

items.name: LOL Match
items.summary: Fullstack MERN platform for League of Legends players
items.description: Fullstack web app (master’s thesis project) to manage summoner profiles, squads, teammate discovery, chat, and stats via the Riot Games API. Two-repo architecture: React + Vite client and Node REST API with MongoDB and real-time WebSockets.
items.context: Riot exposes game data but not a single web space that combines verified signup, LoL profile, squads, player matches, chat, and stats in one responsive experience.
items.impact: Live platform at lolmatch.online where players verify their account, sync their summoner, manage squads, chat in real time, receive notifications, and browse matches and rank without juggling scattered tools outside the browser.
items.role: Fullstack work across lolmatch-frontend and lolmatch-backend: React 19 UI with protected routes and Auth, Socket, Chat, and Notification contexts; Express 5 API with Mongoose, httpOnly JWT cookies, 2FA, and sessions; Socket.io for chat and alerts; Riot API caching, Cloudinary avatars, and Brevo email.
items.platform: React SPA on Netlify (lolmatch.online and legacy Netlify domain) calling a Node.js REST API and WebSockets; local MongoDB in development and MongoDB Atlas in production; multi-domain CORS with cross-origin cookies.
items.scope: MIT thesis project, separate GitHub repos; full auth flows (signup, verification, recovery, 2FA), squads, matches, public and private chat, and Riot integration. Vitest, Cypress, and Jest tests with initial documented coverage.

items.name: Stockearly
items.description: Inventory control app for Etama, a brunch and healthy-food restaurant in Barcelona ([etama.es](https://etama.es)): sales, purchases, and wastage, ingredient catalog, recipes (bill of materials), and a real-time dashboard. Two-repo setup — React + Vite client and event-driven Node API with MongoDB and Socket.io.
items.context: Etama tracked inventory across spreadsheets and manual notes with no single stock view or low-stock alerts; requiring POS or automated invoices on day one would have delayed a tool kitchen and front-of-house could use right away.
items.impact: Panel at stockearly.online deployed for daily restaurant operations: inventory snapshots and low-stock alerts that refresh instantly after each sale, purchase, or wastage event; the public menu and bookings stay on its own website while the kitchen monitors stock in real time. The same core supports POS webhooks and invoices (JSON with preview and confirm, PDF via cloud storage and AI) without redesigning the domain.
items.role: Fullstack development on stock-app-frontend and stock-app-backend: EventBus, stockService, manual endpoints, POS webhook, invoice pipeline, and JWT-backed API; models for ingredients, recipes, suppliers, and notifications. TypeScript client with MUI, Zustand, and react-hook-form; dashboard, ingredients, recipes, and manual logging screens; Socket.io for live snapshots.
items.platform: React SPA (Vite + TypeScript) on Vercel (stockearly.online) calling a Node/Express REST API and WebSockets (port 4000); local or Atlas MongoDB; optional Android packaging with Capacitor.
items.scope: Real client project (Etama, Barcelona): app in production with manual-first flows; ingredient and recipe CRUD, wastage presets, and suppliers. JSON invoices shipped; PDF processing with Gemini/GCS when configured; POS webhook signature verification on the roadmap.

items.name: EMP Website
items.description: Website for Eva Méndez Pérez, a general health psychologist and creator of Terapia de Sentido: migration from WordPress to a monorepo with Next.js 15 (App Router, ISR) and Sanity Studio v3 so she can manage blog posts, services, and pages without plugins.
items.context: The WordPress site had weak SEO, plugin dependency (Amelia for bookings), and cumbersome content upkeep; she needed a fast, Spanish-only professional presence aligned with GDPR, editable by the practitioner herself.
items.impact: Production site at evamendezperez.com with stronger discovery (metadata, JSON-LD, sitemap), migrated blog, online booking via embedded Cal.eu, contact form with Resend and Turnstile, cookie consent, and Studio at studio.evamendezperez.com to update copy and media without code changes.
items.role: Migration and monorepo delivery (pnpm): WordPress and Amelia → Sanity scripts, schemas and GROQ, home, about, blog with Portable Text and carousels, per-service booking pages, contact and legal pages; Tailwind v4 and shadcn/ui; Docker Compose on Coolify (web + studio), CI lint and build, and deployment runbooks.
items.platform: Next.js 15 standalone in Docker (Coolify) at evamendezperez.com; Sanity Studio at studio.evamendezperez.com; Cal.eu (EU) for scheduling; Resend for email; Spanish-only content (Europe/Madrid).
items.scope: Real client project (private repository): phases completed (CMS, pages, SEO, cookies, deploy). Planned phase 6 (Sanity webhook → on-demand ISR and social automation via n8n) documented but not required for current operation.

items.name: IFC Viewer
items.summary: ARQFI web IFC viewer with That Open Engine v3
items.description: Web IFC model viewer (ARQFI IFC Viewer) built with That Open Engine v3, Three.js, and Vite. Layered architecture —domain, application, infrastructure, and presentation— to keep business rules separate from the 3D engine and coordinate models in the browser with no desktop install.
items.context: Reviewing IFC on site or with clients often means desktop viewers or unreliable third-party CDNs and WASM; teams need a lightweight tool that combines the 3D model, properties, and navigation by storeys and categories in one URL.
items.impact: Coordination from viewer.arqfi.com: load a local IFC or bundled sample, inspect properties in table or JSON, isolate and hide by level and category, camera presets and HUD view cube, with WASM and worker assets shipped in the same deployment (no external engine CDN).
items.role: Designed and built the layered viewer: ThatOpenViewerAdapter, IfcLoader and FragmentsManager, Highlighter, Classifier and Hider, property enrichment (quantities, materials, project units), BIM navigation UI, light/dark theme, visualization styles, ViewCubeHud, and postinstall web-ifc pipeline; use-case tests and v0.1.0 release with Conventional Commits.
items.platform: TypeScript SPA (Node 20+) with Vite; deployed on Vercel at viewer.arqfi.com; web-ifc runtime under /vendor/web-ifc and @thatopen/fragments worker bundled in the build.
items.scope: Phases 1 and 2 shipped (v0.1.0, MIT): model load and rebuild, selection and properties, storey and category navigation, camera panel and help; unit tests and typecheck in the local workflow.

items.name: Data Extractor
items.summary: APS cloud extractor for RVT/NWC metadata
items.description: APS Revit Data Extractor: web app to browse Autodesk Docs/ACC and OSS, translate RVT/NWC with Model Derivative, and inspect properties and the model tree with a technical extraction UX. React + Express monorepo with OAuth session handling.
items.context: Pulling properties and metrics from cloud RVT/NWC models means chaining OAuth, Data Management, OSS, translation jobs, and manifest reads by hand; without a unified layer the technical workflow is slow and error-prone.
items.impact: Tool at aps-data.arqfi.com that centralizes sign-in with the user’s Autodesk account, Hub → Project → Content navigation, OSS upload, and extraction with automatic manifest handling (ready, in progress, or queued svf2/svf), plus a property and analytics panel; optional public demo with limits and CAPTCHA to try without credentials.
items.role: Designed and built the monorepo (frontend/ and backend/): 3-legged OAuth, hub routes and Docs extraction, OSS bucket CRUD with signed upload, translate/metadata/properties/tree, smart extraction UI in React, and demoProtection with rate limiting; Docker Compose for dev and prod and documented split deploy (Coolify).
items.platform: React client (port 3001 locally) and Express API (3000) with session cookies; production frontend at aps-data.arqfi.com and API at aps-data-api.arqfi.com; APS Authentication (3-legged and 2-legged), Data Management, OSS, and Model Derivative.
items.scope: MIT monorepo in production: authenticated Docs/ACC flow, app-owned OSS flow (buckets, upload, extract), and configurable public demo (DEMO_URN_BASE64, CAPTCHA); verified with frontend build and backend node --check.

items.name: MSc. Architecture, Design, and Architectural Engineering
items.meta: National University of San Juan, Argentina · 2006 – 2015
items.description: Architect degree (6 years): structural design, construction systems, urban planning, and architectural engineering.

items.name: Master in BIM-Oriented Programming
items.description: ARCH, STR, MEP, and IFC modeling. Scripting with Dynamo and Python. Databases. Revit API.

items.name: Master BIM Software Developer
items.description: BIM web app development with IFC.js and Three.js. Open BIM standards and IFC schema.

items.name: PyRevit and Revit API Course
items.description: Custom tools and automations using the Revit API and the PyRevit framework.

items.name: Master in Fullstack Development
items.description: Web development, software engineering, AI integrations, and automation (n8n, MCP servers). Applied to BIM tool development: REST APIs, database modeling, and front-end interfaces for BIM data.

items.name: Autodesk Authorized Developer
items.meta: Autodesk Developer Network (ADN) member · ID: SEES0029
items.description: Develop integrations, automation, and custom solutions built on Autodesk’s official technologies and APIs.

legalNotice.metadataDescription: Legal notice for marianorluna.com: site owner, terms of use, and intellectual property.
legalNotice.title: Legal notice

sections.title: Website owner
paragraphs: Name or company name: Mariano Luna

sections.title: Terms of use
paragraphs: This website has an informational and professional purpose. Users agree to use its contents appropriately and not for unlawful activities.

sections.title: Intellectual property
paragraphs: The website content, design, and source code belong to the website owner or licensed third parties. Reproduction without permission is prohibited.

privacy.metadataDescription: Privacy policy for marianorluna.com under GDPR: data collected, purpose, retention, and your rights.
privacy.title: Privacy policy

sections.title: Collected data
paragraphs: The contact form collects: name, email, and message. A security token (Cloudflare Turnstile) is also processed to prevent spam.

sections.title: Purpose and legal basis
paragraphs: Purpose: respond to contact requests and potential professional collaborations. Legal basis: user consent (art. 6.1.a GDPR).

sections.title: Recipients and processors
bullets: Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights (if visit and performance measurement is accepted).
Cal.com and YouTube (if third-party cookies are accepted).

sections.title: Retention and rights
paragraphs: Retention period: 1 year. You may exercise access, rectification, erasure, objection, restriction, and portability rights by contacting: contact@marianorluna.com.

cookies.metadataDescription: Cookie policy for marianorluna.com: cookie types, consent, and preference management.
cookies.title: Cookie policy

sections.title: What cookies are
paragraphs: Cookies are small files stored on your device to enable website functionality or remember preferences.

sections.title: Cookies used by this website
bullets: Necessary technical: functional preferences (for example, theme mode and cookie consent).
Analytics (optional): visit and performance measurement with Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights.
Third-party (optional): embedded content from YouTube and Cal.com.

sections.title: How to manage consent
paragraphs: You can accept or reject non-essential cookies from the initial banner or from "Cookie settings" in the site footer.

sections.title: Third-party information
paragraphs: Review each provider's privacy/cookie policies: Vercel, Cloudflare, Cal.com, and YouTube.
